skills.title: Skills
skills.description:
Skills are reusable instruction bundles the agent can invoke from its local tool environment.
          This view shows the current instructions file and the skills currently visible to the local agent runtime.
skills.noInstructionsFile: No instructions file configured for this agent.

configForm.promptTemplateWarning: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Keep it compact and dynamic to avoid recurring token cost and cache churn.
configForm.codexMinimalThinkingWarning: Codex may reject `minimal` thinking when search is enabled.
configForm.bootstrapPromptPlaceholder: Optional initial setup prompt for the first run
configForm.bootstrapPromptInfo: Bootstrap prompt is only sent for fresh sessions. Put stable setup, habits, and longer reusable guidance here. Frequent changes reduce the value of session reuse because new sessions must replay it.
configForm.sessionCompactionInfo: A large cumulative raw token total does not mean the full session is resent on every heartbeat.
configForm.sessionCompactionAgentOverride: This agent has an explicit runtimeConfig session compaction override.
configForm.noThresholdsActive: No Paperclip-managed fresh-session thresholds are active for this adapter.
configForm.thresholdsActive: Paperclip will start a fresh session when one of these thresholds is reached.
configForm.envVarPlaceholder: PAPERCLIP_* variables are injected automatically at runtime.
configForm.name: Name
configForm.title: Title
configForm.capabilitiesPlaceholder: Describe what this agent can do...
configForm.promptTemplatePlaceholder: You are agent {{ agent.name }}. Your role is {{ agent.role }}...
configForm.promptTemplateWarningCreate: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Prefer small task framing and variables like {{ context.* }} or {{ run.* }}; avoid repeating stable instructions here.
configForm.newSecretTooltip: Create secret from current plain value
configForm.sealSecretTooltip: Store value as secret and replace with reference
configForm.nativeSummaryConfirmed: {{adapterLabel}} is treated as natively managing long context, so Paperclip fresh-session rotation defaults to off.
configForm.nativeSummaryLikely: {{adapterLabel}} likely manages long context itself, but Paperclip still keeps conservative rotation defaults for now.
configForm.nativeSummaryUnknown: {{adapterLabel}} does not have verified native compaction behavior, so Paperclip keeps conservative rotation defaults.

pathInstructions.dialogTitle: How to get a full path
pathInstructions.dialogDescription: Paste the absolute path (e.g. /Users/you/project) into the input field.

mac.step1: Open Finder and navigate to the folder.
mac.step3: Hold the Option (⌥) key — "Copy" changes to "Copy as Pathname".
mac.step4: Click "Copy as Pathname", then paste here.
mac.tip: You can also open Terminal, type cd, drag the folder into the terminal window, and press Enter. Then type pwd to see the full path.

windows.step1: Open File Explorer and navigate to the folder.
windows.step2: Click in the address bar at the top — the full path will appear.
windows.step3: Copy the path, then paste here.
windows.tip: Alternatively, hold Shift and right-click the folder, then select "Copy as path".

linux.step1: Open a terminal and navigate to the directory with cd.
linux.step2: Run pwd to print the full path.
linux.step3: Copy the output and paste here.
linux.tip: In most file managers, Ctrl+L reveals the full path in the address bar.
